info.title: FortiManager JSON API Documentation
info.description:
Documentation for FortiManager JSON API, ADOM version 7.0

Policy package manages the different types of policies in device VDOM. All configuration will be copied onto the device during next install.

  • Prefix /pm/pkg/global/ and /pm/pkg/adom/ are used to access or modify the packages and install targets at global or ADOM level.
  • Prefix /pm/config/global/pkg/ and /pm/config/adom/pkg/ are used to access policies inside a package.
  • Prefix /pm/pblock/adom/ and /pm/config/adom/pblock/ are use to configure policy block and policy inside policy block.

This list only contains the policies. All objects that are reference by the policies are shared among the packages, and are listed under ADOM Level Objects.

tags.name: /pm/config/firewall/central-snat-map
tags.description: Configure IPv4 and IPv6 central SNAT policies.

tags.name: /pm/config/firewall/local-in-policy
tags.description: Configure user defined IPv4 local-in policies.

tags.name: /pm/config/firewall/local-in-policy6
tags.description: Configure user defined IPv6 local-in policies.

tags.name: /pm/config/firewall/policy
tags.description:
Configure IPv4/IPv6 policies. When configuring policy within policy package, use (/pm/config/adom/pkg/).
When configuring policy for a policy block, use (/pm/config/adom/pblock/).

tags.name: /pm/config/user/nac-policy
tags.description: Configure NAC policy matching pattern to identify matching NAC devices.

tags.name: /pm/pkg
tags.description: Policy package or folder. The full path to the package, including package name and any parent folders, can be specified as the name in the URL.

tags.name: /pm/pkg/schedule
tags.description:
Installation schedule of a policy package to devices.
When adding schedule in workspace mode, the ADOM should be committed first.

common.filter.object.description: Filter the result according to a set of criteria.

common.range.object.description: Limit the number of output. For a range of [a, n], the output will contain n elements, starting from the ath matching result.

common.sort.object.description: Specify the sorting of the returned result.

policyid.description: User defined local in policy ID.
User defined local in policy ID.

params.cdb.get.obj.option.opts.description:
Set fetch option for the request. If no option is specified, by default the attributes of the object will be returned.
scope member - Return a list of scope members along with other attributes.
chksum - Return the check-sum value instead of attributes.
datasrc - Return all objects that can be referenced by an attribute. Require attr parameter.

params.cdb.get.table.option.opts.description:
Set fetch option for the request. If no option is specified, by default the attributes of the objects will be returned.
count - Return the number of matching entries instead of the actual entry data.
scope member - Return a list of scope members along with other attributes.
datasrc - Return all objects that can be referenced by an attribute. Require attr parameter.
get reserved - Also return reserved objects in the result.
syntax - Return the attribute syntax of a table or an object, instead of the actual entry data. All filter parameters will be ignored.

items.description: Select an IPv4 destination address from available options. Required for web proxy authentication.

dstaddr.description: Select an IPv4 destination address from available options. Required for web proxy authentication.

items.description: Select an IPv6 destination address from available options. Required for web proxy authentication.

dstaddr6.description: Select an IPv6 destination address from available options. Required for web proxy authentication.

ip-based.description: Enable/disable IP-based authentication. When enabled, previously authenticated users from the same IP address will be exempted.

protocol.description: Authentication is required for the selected protocol (default = HTTP).

items.description: Authentication is required for the selected IPv4 source address.

srcaddr.description: Authentication is required for the selected IPv4 source address.

items.description: Authentication is required for the selected IPv6 source address.

srcaddr6.description: Authentication is required for the selected IPv6 source address.

sso-auth-method.description: Select a single-sign on (SSO) authentication method.

web-portal.description: Enable/disable web portal for proxy transparent policy (default = enable).

auth-https.description: Enable/disable redirecting HTTP user authentication to HTTPS.

captive-portal-ssl-port.description: Captive portal SSL port number (1 - 65535, default = 7831).

cert-auth.description: Enable/disable redirecting certificate authentication to HTTPS portal.

cert-captive-portal-port.description: Certificate captive portal port number (1 - 65535, default = 7832).

items.description: Address range for the IP based device query.

dev-range.description: Address range for the IP based device query.

items.description: CA certificate used for client certificate verification.

user-cert-ca.description: CA certificate used for client certificate verification.

interface.description: Incoming interface name from available interfaces.

srcaddr.description: Source address name from available addresses.

action.description: Action taken when the threshold is reached.

quarantine-expiry.description: Duration of quarantine. (Format ###d##h##m, minimum 1m, maximum 364d23h59m, default = 5m). Requires quarantine set to attacker.

threshold.description: Anomaly threshold. Number of detected instances (packets per second or concurrent session number) that triggers the anomaly action.

_policy_block.description:
Assigned policy block. When this attribute is set, the policy represent a policy block, and all other attributes are ignored.
This attribute is not available when configuring policy inside a policy block.

auth-cert.description: HTTPS server certificate for policy authentication.

auth-redirect-addr.description: HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect address for firewall authentication.

av-profile.description: Name of an existing Antivirus profile.

captive-portal-exempt.description: Enable to exempt some users from the captive portal.

items.description: Custom fields to append to log messages for this policy.

custom-log-fields.description: Custom fields to append to log messages for this policy.

delay-tcp-npu-session.description: Enable TCP NPU session delay to guarantee packet order of 3-way handshake.

diffserv-forward.description: Enable to change packet's DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-forward value.

diffserv-reverse.description: Enable to change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-rev value.

diffservcode-forward.description: Change packet's DiffServ to this value.

diffservcode-rev.description: Change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ to this value.

dsri.description: Enable DSRI to ignore HTTP server responses.

dstaddr.description: Destination IPv4 address and address group names.

dstaddr-negate.description: When enabled dstaddr/dstaddr6 specifies what the destination address must NOT be.

dstaddr6.description: Destination IPv6 address name and address group names.

dynamic-shaping.description: Enable/disable dynamic RADIUS defined traffic shaping.

firewall-session-dirty.description: How to handle sessions if the configuration of this firewall policy changes.

fixedport.description: Enable to prevent source NAT from changing a session's source port.

fsso-agent-for-ntlm.description: FSSO agent to use for NTLM authentication.

geoip-anycast.description: Enable/disable recognition of anycast IP addresses using the geography IP database.

geoip-match.description: Match geography address based either on its physical location or registered location.

global-label.description: Label for the policy that appears when the GUI is in Global View mode.

items.description: Names of user groups that can authenticate with this policy.

groups.description: Names of user groups that can authenticate with this policy.

http-policy-redirect.description: Redirect HTTP(S) traffic to matching transparent web proxy policy.

inbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: only traffic from the remote network can initiate a VPN.

inspection-mode.description: Policy inspection mode (Flow/proxy). Default is Flow mode.

internet-service.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services for this policy. If enabled, destination address and service are not used.

internet-service-negate.description: When enabled internet-service specifies what the service must NOT be.

internet-service-src.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services in source for this policy. If enabled, source address is not used.

items.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

internet-service-src-custom-group.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

internet-service-src-negate.description: When enabled internet-service-src specifies what the service must NOT be.

ippool.description: Enable to use IP Pools for source NAT.

label.description: Label for the policy that appears when the GUI is in Section View mode.

logtraffic.description: Enable or disable logging. Log all sessions or security profile sessions.

logtraffic-start.description: Record logs when a session starts.

match-vip.description: Enable to match packets that have had their destination addresses changed by a VIP.

match-vip-only.description: Enable/disable matching of only those packets that have had their destination addresses changed by a VIP.

natinbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: apply destination NAT to inbound traffic.

natip.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: source NAT IP address for outgoing traffic.

natoutbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: apply source NAT to outbound traffic.

outbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: only traffic from the internal network can initiate a VPN.

passive-wan-health-measurement.description: Enable/disable passive WAN health measurement. When enabled, auto-asic-offload is disabled.

permit-any-host.description: Accept UDP packets from any host.

permit-stun-host.description: Accept UDP packets from any Session Traversal Utilities for NAT (STUN) host.

profile-type.description: Determine whether the firewall policy allows security profile groups or single profiles only.

radius-mac-auth-bypass.description: Enable MAC authentication bypass. The bypassed MAC address must be received from RADIUS server.

redirect-url.description: URL users are directed to after seeing and accepting the disclaimer or authenticating.

replacemsg-override-group.description: Override the default replacement message group for this policy.

reputation-direction.description: Direction of the initial traffic for reputation to take effect.

items.description: Address names if this is an RTP NAT policy.

rtp-addr.description: Address names if this is an RTP NAT policy.

schedule-timeout.description: Enable to force current sessions to end when the schedule object times out. Disable allows them to end from inactivity.

send-deny-packet.description: Enable to send a reply when a session is denied or blocked by a firewall policy.

service-negate.description: When enabled service specifies what the service must NOT be.

session-ttl.description: TTL in seconds for sessions accepted by this policy (0 means use the system default session TTL).

srcaddr.description: Source IPv4 address and address group names.

srcaddr-negate.description: When enabled srcaddr/srcaddr6 specifies what the source address must NOT be.

ssh-policy-redirect.description: Redirect SSH traffic to matching transparent proxy policy.

timeout-send-rst.description: Enable/disable sending RST packets when TCP sessions expire.

tos.description: ToS (Type of Service) value used for comparison.

tos-mask.description: Non-zero bit positions are used for comparison while zero bit positions are ignored.

auth-redirect-addr.description: HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect address for firewall authentication.

av-profile.description: Name of an existing Antivirus profile.

captive-portal-exempt.description: Enable to exempt some users from the captive portal.

items.description: Custom fields to append to log messages for this policy.

custom-log-fields.description: Custom fields to append to log messages for this policy.

delay-tcp-npu-session.description: Enable TCP NPU session delay to guarantee packet order of 3-way handshake.

diffserv-forward.description: Enable to change packet's DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-forward value.

diffserv-reverse.description: Enable to change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-rev value.

diffservcode-forward.description: Change packet's DiffServ to this value.

diffservcode-rev.description: Change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ to this value.

dlp-sensor.description: Name of an existing DLP sensor.

dnsfilter-profile.description: Name of an existing DNS filter profile.

dsri.description: Enable DSRI to ignore HTTP server responses.

items.description: Destination IPv4 address and address group names.

dstaddr.description: Destination IPv4 address and address group names.

dstaddr-negate.description: When enabled dstaddr/dstaddr6 specifies what the destination address must NOT be.

items.description: Destination IPv6 address name and address group names.

dstaddr6.description: Destination IPv6 address name and address group names.

dynamic-shaping.description: Enable/disable dynamic RADIUS defined traffic shaping.

emailfilter-profile.description: Name of an existing email filter profile.

fec.description: Enable/disable Forward Error Correction on traffic matching this policy on a FEC device.

file-filter-profile.description: Name of an existing file-filter profile.

firewall-session-dirty.description: How to handle sessions if the configuration of this firewall policy changes.

fixedport.description: Enable to prevent source NAT from changing a session's source port.

fsso-agent-for-ntlm.description: FSSO agent to use for NTLM authentication.

geoip-anycast.description: Enable/disable recognition of anycast IP addresses using the geography IP database.

geoip-match.description: Match geography address based either on its physical location or registered location.

global-label.description: Label for the policy that appears when the GUI is in Global View mode.

items.description: Names of user groups that can authenticate with this policy.

groups.description: Names of user groups that can authenticate with this policy.

http-policy-redirect.description: Redirect HTTP(S) traffic to matching transparent web proxy policy.

icap-profile.description: Name of an existing ICAP profile.

inbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: only traffic from the remote network can initiate a VPN.

inspection-mode.description: Policy inspection mode (Flow/proxy). Default is Flow mode.

internet-service.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services for this policy. If enabled, destination address and service are not used.

internet-service-negate.description: When enabled internet-service specifies what the service must NOT be.

internet-service-src.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services in source for this policy. If enabled, source address is not used.

items.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

internet-service-src-custom-group.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

internet-service-src-negate.description: When enabled internet-service-src specifies what the service must NOT be.

ippool.description: Enable to use IP Pools for source NAT.

ips-sensor.description: Name of an existing IPS sensor.

label.description: Label for the policy that appears when the GUI is in Section View mode.

logtraffic.description: Enable or disable logging. Log all sessions or security profile sessions.

logtraffic-start.description: Record logs when a session starts.

match-vip.description: Enable to match packets that have had their destination addresses changed by a VIP.

match-vip-only.description: Enable/disable matching of only those packets that have had their destination addresses changed by a VIP.

natinbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: apply destination NAT to inbound traffic.

natip.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: source NAT IP address for outgoing traffic.

natoutbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: apply source NAT to outbound traffic.

outbound.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: only traffic from the internal network can initiate a VPN.

passive-wan-health-measurement.description: Enable/disable passive WAN health measurement. When enabled, auto-asic-offload is disabled.

permit-any-host.description: Accept UDP packets from any host.

permit-stun-host.description: Accept UDP packets from any Session Traversal Utilities for NAT (STUN) host.

policy-offload.description: Enable/Disable hardware session setup for CGNAT.

profile-protocol-options.description: Name of an existing Protocol options profile.

profile-type.description: Determine whether the firewall policy allows security profile groups or single profiles only.

radius-mac-auth-bypass.description: Enable MAC authentication bypass. The bypassed MAC address must be received from RADIUS server.

redirect-url.description: URL users are directed to after seeing and accepting the disclaimer or authenticating.

replacemsg-override-group.description: Override the default replacement message group for this policy.

reputation-direction.description: Direction of the initial traffic for reputation to take effect.

items.description: Address names if this is an RTP NAT policy.

rtp-addr.description: Address names if this is an RTP NAT policy.

schedule-timeout.description: Enable to force current sessions to end when the schedule object times out. Disable allows them to end from inactivity.

sctp-filter-profile.description: Name of an existing SCTP filter profile.

send-deny-packet.description: Enable to send a reply when a session is denied or blocked by a firewall policy.

service-negate.description: When enabled service specifies what the service must NOT be.

session-ttl.description: TTL in seconds for sessions accepted by this policy (0 means use the system default session TTL).

items.description: Source IPv4 address and address group names.

srcaddr.description: Source IPv4 address and address group names.

srcaddr-negate.description: When enabled srcaddr/srcaddr6 specifies what the source address must NOT be.

items.description: Source IPv6 address name and address group names.

srcaddr6.description: Source IPv6 address name and address group names.

ssh-filter-profile.description: Name of an existing SSH filter profile.

ssh-policy-redirect.description: Redirect SSH traffic to matching transparent proxy policy.

ssl-ssh-profile.description: Name of an existing SSL SSH profile.

tcp-session-without-syn.description: Enable/disable creation of TCP session without SYN flag.

timeout-send-rst.description: Enable/disable sending RST packets when TCP sessions expire.

tos.description: ToS (Type of Service) value used for comparison.

tos-mask.description: Non-zero bit positions are used for comparison while zero bit positions are ignored.

items.description: Names of individual users that can authenticate with this policy.

users.description: Names of individual users that can authenticate with this policy.

utm-status.description: Enable to add one or more security profiles (AV, IPS, etc.) to the firewall policy.

uuid.description: Universally Unique Identifier (UUID; automatically assigned but can be manually reset).

videofilter-profile.description: Name of an existing VideoFilter profile.

vlan-cos-fwd.description: VLAN forward direction user priority: 255 passthrough, 0 lowest, 7 highest.

vlan-cos-rev.description: VLAN reverse direction user priority: 255 passthrough, 0 lowest, 7 highest.

voip-profile.description: Name of an existing VoIP profile.

vpntunnel.description: Policy-based IPsec VPN: name of the IPsec VPN Phase 1.

waf-profile.description: Name of an existing Web application firewall profile.

wanopt-passive-opt.description: WAN optimization passive mode options. This option decides what IP address will be used to connect server.

wccp.description: Enable/disable forwarding traffic matching this policy to a configured WCCP server.

webfilter-profile.description: Name of an existing Web filter profile.

items.description: IDs of one or more application categories that this shaper applies application control traffic shaping to.

app-category.description: IDs of one or more application categories that this shaper applies application control traffic shaping to.

items.description: One or more application group names.

app-group.description: One or more application group names.

application.description: IDs of one or more applications that this shaper applies application control traffic shaping to.

diffserv-forward.description: Enable to change packet's DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-forward value.

diffserv-reverse.description: Enable to change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ values to the specified diffservcode-rev value.

diffservcode-forward.description: Change packet's DiffServ to this value.

diffservcode-rev.description: Change packet's reverse (reply) DiffServ to this value.

items.description: IPv4 destination address and address group names.

dstaddr.description: IPv4 destination address and address group names.

items.description: IPv6 destination address and address group names.

dstaddr6.description: IPv6 destination address and address group names.

items.description: Apply this traffic shaping policy to user groups that have authenticated with the FortiGate.

groups.description: Apply this traffic shaping policy to user groups that have authenticated with the FortiGate.

internet-service.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services for this policy. If enabled, destination address and service are not used.

internet-service-src.description: Enable/disable use of Internet Services in source for this policy. If enabled, source address is not used.

items.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

internet-service-src-custom-group.description: Custom Internet Service source group name.

ip-version.description: Apply this traffic shaping policy to IPv4 or IPv6 traffic.

per-ip-shaper.description: Per-IP traffic shaper to apply with this policy.

items.description: IPv4 source address and address group names.

srcaddr.description: IPv4 source address and address group names.

items.description: IPv6 source address and address group names.

srcaddr6.description: IPv6 source address and address group names.

tos.description: ToS (Type of Service) value used for comparison.

tos-mask.description: Non-zero bit positions are used for comparison while zero bit positions are ignored.

traffic-shaper.description: Traffic shaper to apply to traffic forwarded by the firewall policy.

traffic-shaper-reverse.description: Traffic shaper to apply to response traffic received by the firewall policy.

items.description: IDs of one or more FortiGuard Web Filtering categories that this shaper applies traffic shaping to.

url-category.description: IDs of one or more FortiGuard Web Filtering categories that this shaper applies traffic shaping to.

items.description: Apply this traffic shaping policy to individual users that have authenticated with the FortiGate.

users.description: Apply this traffic shaping policy to individual users that have authenticated with the FortiGate.

description.description: Description for the NAC policy matching pattern.

family.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching family.

hw-vendor.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching hardware vendor.

mac.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching MAC address.

os.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching operating system.

ssid-policy.description: SSID policy to be applied on the matched NAC policy.

type.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching type.

user.description: Support meta variable NAC policy matching user.

name.description: Does not include any parent folders.

subobj.description: Available if object type is folder, includes all policy packages and sub-folders under the folder object.

ssl-ssh-profile.description: SSL-SSH profile required for NGFW-mode policy package.

adom_rev_comment.description: The comment for the new ADOM revision. It will be ignored in add request if adom_rev_name is not set.

adom_rev_name.description: The name for the new ADOM revision. If not set in add request, no ADOM revision will be created during installation.

datetime.description:
Indicates the date and time of the schedule.
It should follow the following format: "YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm".
The value of this attribute should not be earlier than the current time of the system.

dev_rev_comment.description: The comment for the device configuration revision that will be generated during install.
